seoTitle: Maddens Plains vs Magenta — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest

ogTitle: Maddens Plains vs Magenta: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Maddens Plains and Magenta.
heading: Maddens Plains vs Magenta — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Maddens Plains, New South Wales and Magenta, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Magenta has a lower overall climate risk score (42/100) compared to Maddens Plains (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Maddens Plains or Magenta safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Maddens Plains has a flood risk score of 28/100, while Magenta scores 48/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Maddens Plains scores 57/100 for bushfire risk. Magenta scores 57/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Maddens Plains or Magenta?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Magenta has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.98% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
